name: Moghlai Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove skin from the chicken. Cut into serving pieces, cutting whole legs into two pieces and whole breasts into six pieces.
2. Spread the chicken in a single layer and sprinkle with 1/4 tsp salt and black pepper to taste. Pat the seasoning onto the chicken to help it adhere. Turn the chicken over and repeat with another 1/4 tsp salt and black pepper to taste.
3. Heat oil and butter in a large skillet, preferably non-stick, over medium-high heat. When hot, add cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaves, and as many chicken pieces as the pan will hold in a single layer (do not crowd). Brown the chicken on both sides; transfer with a slotted spoon to an ovenproof casserole.
4. Repeat with remaining chicken.
5. Add almonds to hot oil in the skillet and stir. As soon as they begin to brown, about 1 minute, stir in raisins. Immediately pour the contents of the skillet, fat and all over the chicken.
6. In a small bowl, stir together yogurt, ground cumin, cayenne, remaining salt, and black pepper to taste. Pour over the chicken; mix well.
7. Cover the casserole and place in a preheated 350°F oven for 20 minutes. Turn the chicken pieces over; baste with juices. Cover and return to the oven for another 20 to 25 minutes, or until the chicken is tender.
8. Just before serving, if necessary, reheat the chicken over low heat. Remove chicken pieces and transfer to a warm serving dish. Spoon off most of the fat left in the casserole; discard. Place the remaining sauce back into the casserole and cook on medium-high heat until thickened, about 5 minutes. Pour the thickened sauce over the chicken.
Serves: 6
